title: Fatty Juicy Ethiopian Lamb Stew
description: A rich and flavorful Ethiopian lamb stew featuring shoyu-koji and spherified ingredients, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and friendly to shojin dietary preferences.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the shoyu-koji, berbere spice, and sodium alginate, and stir to combine.
Add the water and lamb broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours.
Use a slotted spoon to remove the lamb from the pot, and set it aside to rest.
Strain the cooking liquid through a fine mesh sieve into a clean pot, and discard the solids.
Return the lamb to the pot, and simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.
